generatedSentence: The exotic autumn landscape, often viewed as art, felt dull to the narcissistic inventor, Kemp, whose idealism invests in tweed and dictionaries. His true moniker, "The Vandyke Visionary," was because his burrow housed an aquarium of platypus and hydrangea specimens. He aimed for ultimate snowflake symmetry, observing from upwind during nights, dreaming beyond horseback oats, occasionally telling a bedtime story to his munchkin apprentice, always bored by conventional fact and seeking a catalyst for change. Unbeknownst to him, an outrageous, manic conflict brewed, as rogue archers, making unilateral decisions, initiated a landslide of chaos, intentionally causing a premature set of endings. A demented figure, known for murders and an unsecure network, pwned into the system, leaving the entire city implicated. This parasite orchestrated a barrage of issues from hidden mines and milling factories, creating a jilted, unfair atmosphere because of his controlling grip, like a ticking bomb over the city’s humble barbecues. Suddenly, an ultrasonic pulse, a fluke of undefeatable energy, caused the very domes of the city to jangle. Kemp, now astride his mechanical griffin, performed stunning acrobatics, whisking through the air. He deployed a paste-like lacquer, an aura of protection against the narcotic-laced bottles now being dropped by the orcs from their omnibus. This act, an unpopular promotion of his defiance, was viewed by the rockfish tanks below, a dumb but necessary act to protect the primacy of his domain. He even found time for a quick wink. The ensuing bloop, a muted explosion, meant the abattoir of chaos was ending. The key pickers, the homeless from the rinks, and even the deaf and dull grackles, watched as the voltmeter settled. Kemp, now unstuck and uplifted, received notifications on his wrist, a promotion for his ingenious employ of fertilizers to repair the devastated lakeside. Though his efforts to restore the billed backing of the factories were immense, he now had a fair chance, not just another set of nicks, and his views of the vesper future were bright.
panelSentences: The exotic autumn landscape, often viewed as art, felt dull to the narcissistic inventor, Kemp, whose idealism invests in tweed and dictionaries. His true moniker, "The Vandyke Visionary," was because his burrow housed an aquarium of platypus and hydrangea specimens. He aimed for ultimate snowflake symmetry, observing from upwind during nights, dreaming beyond horseback oats, occasionally telling a bedtime story to his munchkin apprentice, always bored by conventional fact and seeking a catalyst for change.

Unbeknownst to him, an outrageous, manic conflict brewed, as rogue archers, making unilateral decisions, initiated a landslide of chaos, intentionally causing a premature set of endings. A demented figure, known for murders and an unsecure network, pwned into the system, leaving the entire city implicated. This parasite orchestrated a barrage of issues from hidden mines and milling factories, creating a jilted, unfair atmosphere because of his controlling grip, like a ticking bomb over the city’s humble barbecues.

Suddenly, an ultrasonic pulse, a fluke of undefeatable energy, caused the very domes of the city to jangle. Kemp, now astride his mechanical griffin, performed stunning acrobatics, whisking through the air. He deployed a paste-like lacquer, an aura of protection against the narcotic-laced bottles now being dropped by the orcs from their omnibus. This act, an unpopular promotion of his defiance, was viewed by the rockfish tanks below, a dumb but necessary act to protect the primacy of his domain. He even found time for a quick wink.

The ensuing bloop, a muted explosion, meant the abattoir of chaos was ending. The key pickers, the homeless from the rinks, and even the deaf and dull grackles, watched as the voltmeter settled. Kemp, now unstuck and uplifted, received notifications on his wrist, a promotion for his ingenious employ of fertilizers to repair the devastated lakeside. Though his efforts to restore the billed backing of the factories were immense, he now had a fair chance, not just another set of nicks, and his views of the vesper future were bright.
rawPanelSentences: The exotic autumn landscape, often viewed as art, felt dull to the narcissistic inventor, Kemp, whose idealism invests in tweed and dictionaries. His true moniker, "The Vandyke Visionary," was because his burrow housed an aquarium of platypus and hydrangea specimens. He aimed for ultimate snowflake symmetry, observing from upwind during nights, dreaming beyond horseback oats, occasionally telling a bedtime story to his munchkin apprentice, always bored by conventional fact and seeking a catalyst for change.

Unbeknownst to him, an outrageous, manic conflict brewed, as rogue archers, making unilateral decisions, initiated a landslide of chaos, intentionally causing a premature set of endings. A demented figure, known for murders and an unsecure network, pwned into the system, leaving the entire city implicated. This parasite orchestrated a barrage of issues from hidden mines and milling factories, creating a jilted, unfair atmosphere because of his controlling grip, like a ticking bomb over the city’s humble barbecues.

Suddenly, an ultrasonic pulse, a fluke of undefeatable energy, caused the very domes of the city to jangle. Kemp, now astride his mechanical griffin, performed stunning acrobatics, whisking through the air. He deployed a paste-like lacquer, an aura of protection against the narcotic-laced bottles now being dropped by the orcs from their omnibus. This act, an unpopular promotion of his defiance, was viewed by the rockfish tanks below, a dumb but necessary act to protect the primacy of his domain. He even found time for a quick wink.

The ensuing bloop, a muted explosion, meant the abattoir of chaos was ending. The key pickers, the homeless from the rinks, and even the deaf and dull grackles, watched as the voltmeter settled. Kemp, now unstuck and uplifted, received notifications on his wrist, a promotion for his ingenious employ of fertilizers to repair the devastated lakeside. Though his efforts to restore the billed backing of the factories were immense, he now had a fair chance, not just another set of nicks, and his views of the vesper future were bright.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a scarlet-haired inventor wearing a silver chrome helmet and riding a mechanical griffin with glowing emerald eyes. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A scarlet-haired inventor studies a holographic snowflake in his high-tech burrow filled with exotic plants and strange creatures, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: An expansive, futuristic cityscape, with domes under attack by shadowy archers, wide shot, chaos emanating from distant factories. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The scarlet-haired inventor, riding his mechanical griffin, deploys a shimmering paste from a device, fending off airborne orc vehicles, dynamic action shot from below. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The inventor, now grounded, receives a holographic notification on his wrist, standing beside a restored lakeside with a peaceful cityscape in the background, eye-level shot.

definitions.definition: An organism that is exotic to an environment.
An exotic dancer; a stripteaser.
Any exotic particle.
definitions.example: Glueballs, theoretical particles composed only of gluons, are exotics.

definitions.definition: Foreign, especially in an exciting way.
definitions.example: an exotic appearance

definitions.definition: Non-native to the ecosystem.
Being or relating to an option with features that make it more complex than commonly traded options.
A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed in the process.
Someone or something that encourages progress or change.
definitions.example: Economic development and integration are working as a catalyst for peace.

definitions.definition: An inciting incident that sets the successive conflict into motion.
A catalytic converter.
A public slaughterhouse for cattle, sheep, etc.
A place likened to a slaughterhouse.

The conscious production or arrangement of sounds, colours, forms, movements, or other elements in a manner that affects the senses and emotions, usually specifically the production of the beautiful in a graphic or plastic medium.
definitions.example: There is a debate as to whether graffiti is art or vandalism.

definitions.definition: The creative and emotional expression of mental imagery, such as visual, auditory, social, etc.
Skillful creative activity, usually with an aesthetic focus.
definitions.example: She's mastered the art of programming.

definitions.definition: The study and the product of these processes.
definitions.example: He's at university to study art.

definitions.definition: Aesthetic value.
definitions.example: Her photographs are nice, but there's no art in them.

definitions.definition: Artwork.
definitions.example: Sotheby's regularly auctions art for millions.

definitions.definition: A field or category of art, such as painting, sculpture, music, ballet, or literature.
definitions.example: I'm a great supporter of the arts.

definitions.definition: A nonscientific branch of learning; one of the liberal arts.
Skill that is attained by study, practice, or observation.
Contrivance, scheming, manipulation.

Traditionally the third of the four seasons, when deciduous trees lose their leaves; typically regarded as being from September 24 to December 22 in parts of the Northern Hemisphere, and the months of March, April and May in the Southern Hemisphere.
(by extension) The time period when someone or something is past its prime.
A person with relatively dark hair and a warm skin tone, seen as best suited to certain colours in clothing.
Of or relating to autumn; autumnal
definitions.example: autumn leaves

definitions.definition: (usually with on, upon, etc.) The back of a horse.
A ridge of sand, gravel, and boulders.
On the back of a horse.
definitions.example: He rode horseback into town.

definitions.definition: Any of several shrubs, of the genus Hydrangea, having large clusters of white, pink or blue flowers
Coarse, rough hair, wool, or fur; (in the plural) knotty hairs that will not felt.
For the reason (that).
On account (of), for sake (of).
definitions.example: My life is ruined because of you!

definitions.definition: (by ellipsis) Used alone to refuse to provide a full answer a question begun with "why", usually taken as an anapodoton of the elided full phrase "Because I said so".
On account of, because of.
By or for the cause that; on this account that; for the reason that.
definitions.example: I hid myself because I was afraid.

definitions.definition: As is known, inferred, or determined from the fact that.
definitions.example: I don't think he is a nice person, because he yells at people for no reason.

definitions.definition: So that, in order that.
The time or hour at which one retires to bed in order to sleep.
A mountain.
A hill.
A mound of earth and stones raised over a grave or graves.
A heap of rubbish, attle, or other such refuse.
A small vehicle used to carry a load and pulled or pushed by hand.
(saltworks) A wicker case in which salt is put to drain.
A castrated boar.
A long sleeveless flannel garment for infants.
A fortified town.
A town or city.
A town having a municipal corporation and certain traditional rights.
An administrative district in some cities, e.g., London.

An administrative unit of a city which, under most circumstances according to state or national law, would be considered a larger or more powerful entity; most commonly used in American English to define the five counties that make up New York City.
Other similar administrative units in cities and states in various parts of the world.
A district in Alaska having powers similar to a county.
An association of men who gave pledges or sureties to the king for the good behaviour of each other.
The pledge or surety thus given.
A tunnel or hole, often as dug by a small creature.
A heap or heaps of rubbish or refuse.
To dig a tunnel or hole
(with adverbial of direction) to move underneath or press up against in search of safety or comfort
definitions.example: The young girl burrowed into the bed.

definitions.definition: (with into) to investigate thoroughly
definitions.example: The journalist burrowed into the origins of the mayor's corruption.

definitions.definition: Something actual as opposed to invented.
definitions.example: In this story, the Gettysburg Address is a fact, but the rest is fiction.

definitions.definition: Something which is real.
definitions.example: Gravity is a fact, not a theory.

definitions.definition: Something concrete used as a basis for further interpretation.
definitions.example: Let's look at the facts of the case before deciding.

definitions.definition: An objective consensus on a fundamental reality that has been agreed upon by a substantial number of experts.
definitions.example: There is no doubting the fact that the Earth orbits the Sun.

definitions.definition: Information about a particular subject, especially actual conditions and/or circumstances.
definitions.example: The facts about space travel.

definitions.definition: An individual value or measurement at the lowest level of granularity in a data warehouse.
Action; the realm of action.
A wrongful or criminal deed.
definitions.example: He had become an accessory after the fact.

definitions.definition: A feat or meritorious deed.
Used before making a statement to introduce it as a trustworthy one.
(acoustics) Beyond (higher in frequency than) the range of sound perceptible to the human ear; with a frequency of 20 kilohertz or higher.
A crystal of snow, having approximate hexagonal symmetry.
Any of several bulbous European plants, of the genus Leucojum, having white flowers.
The snow bunting, Plectrophenax nivalis.
Someone who believes they are as unique and special as a snowflake.
Someone hypersensitive to insult or offense, especially a young person with politically correct sensibilities.
Someone (usually white) who was opposed to the abolition of slavery (Missouri, 1860s)
A type of lesion that appears as scattered white-brown spots under high magnification light microscopy.
Something that is unique in every presentation.
(usually derogative) A Caucasian person.
To fall in the manner of a snowflake.
To arrange (data) into a snowflake schema.
Exact correspondence on either side of a dividing line, plane, center or axis.
The satisfying arrangement of a balanced distribution of the elements of a whole.
A deaf person.
(with "the") Those who are deaf, taken as a group.
To deafen.
Unable to hear, or only partially able to hear.
Unwilling to listen or be persuaded; determinedly inattentive; regardless.
definitions.example: Those people are deaf to reason.

definitions.definition: Obscurely heard; stifled; deadened.
Decayed; tasteless; dead.
definitions.example: a deaf nut; deaf corn

definitions.definition: To render dull; to remove or blunt an edge or something that was sharp.
definitions.example: Years of misuse have dulled the tools.

definitions.definition: To soften, moderate or blunt; to make dull, stupid, or sluggish; to stupefy.
definitions.example: He drinks to dull the pain.

definitions.definition: To lose a sharp edge; to become dull.
definitions.example: A razor will dull with use.

definitions.definition: To render dim or obscure; to sully; to tarnish.
Lacking the ability to cut easily; not sharp.
definitions.example: All these knives are dull.

definitions.definition: Boring; not exciting or interesting.
definitions.example: He sat through the dull lecture and barely stayed awake.

definitions.definition: Not shiny; having a matte finish or no particular luster or brightness.
definitions.example: Choose a dull finish to hide fingerprints.

definitions.definition: Not bright or intelligent; stupid; having slow understanding.
Sluggish, listless.
Cloudy, overcast.
definitions.example: It's a dull day.

definitions.definition: Insensible; unfeeling.
Heavy; lifeless; inert.
(of pain etc) Not intense; felt indistinctly or only slightly.
definitions.example: Pressing on the bruise produces a dull pain.

definitions.definition: (of a noise or sound) Not clear, muffled.

A reference work with a list of words from one or more languages, normally ordered alphabetically, explaining each word's meaning, and sometimes containing information on its etymology, pronunciation, usage, translations, and other data.
(preceded by the) A synchronic dictionary of a standardised language held to only contain words that are properly part of the language.
(by extension) Any work that has a list of material organized alphabetically; e.g., biographical dictionary, encyclopedic dictionary.
An associative array, a data structure where each value is referenced by a particular key, analogous to words and definitions in a physical dictionary.
To look up in a dictionary.
To add to a dictionary.
To compile a dictionary.
A natural disaster that involves the breakup and downhill flow of rock, mud, water and anything caught in the path.
A vote won by a wide or overwhelming majority.
definitions.example: The candidate won by a landslide.

definitions.definition: To undergo a landslide.
An edge with ornamental triangular points.
A style of facial hair which has both a mustache and goatee but with all cheek hair shaven.
A style of dress or collar similar to those in Anthony van Dyck's portrait paintings; a small round cape, the border ornamented with points and indentations.
To fit or furnish with a vandyke; to form with points or scallops like a vandyke.
A personal name or nickname; an informal label, often drawing attention to a particular attribute.
definitions.example: The rookie was upset at being called Lemon Drop until she realized that everyone on the team had a silly moniker.

definitions.definition: A signature.
An object (structured item of data) used to associate the name of an object with its location.
The crime of deliberately killing another person without justification.
definitions.example: The defendant was charged with murder.

definitions.definition: The act of deliberate killing of another person or other being without justification, especially with malice aforethought.
definitions.example: There have been ten unsolved murders this year alone.

definitions.definition: (in jurisdictions which use the felony murder rule) The commission of an act which abets the commission of a crime the commission of which causes the death of a human.
(used as a predicative noun) Something terrible to endure.
definitions.example: This headache is murder.

definitions.definition: (collective) A group of crows; the collective noun for crows.
To deliberately kill (a person or persons) without justification, especially with malice aforethought.
definitions.example: The woman found dead in her kitchen was murdered by her husband.

definitions.definition: To defeat decisively.
definitions.example: Our team is going to murder them.

definitions.definition: To kick someone's ass or chew someone out (used to express one’s anger at somebody).
definitions.example: He's torn my best shirt. When I see him, I'll murder him!

definitions.definition: To botch or mangle.
To devour, ravish.
definitions.example: I could murder a hamburger right now.

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to someone who exhibits mania or craziness; wicked.
Suffering from mania, the state of an abnormally elevated or irritable mood, arousal, and/or energy levels.
Intended or planned; done deliberately or voluntarily.
Done with intent.
Any of several large, ferocious sea creatures, now especially the killer whale.
A mythical evil monstrous humanoid creature, usually quite aggressive and often green.
A porcine humanoid monster larger than humans, sometimes pink. (found in Japanese pop-culture and called "orc" when imported to the West)
A low-pitched beeping sound.
definitions.example: The robot produced a series of beeps and bloops before giving its answer to the problem.

definitions.definition: To make a hit just beyond the infield.
To produce a low-pitched beeping sound.
To cover up splices in a soundtrack tape to eliminate the unwanted noise they may produce.
A fireplace or pit for grilling food, typically used outdoors and traditionally employing hot charcoal as the heating medium.
definitions.example: We cooked our food on the barbecue.

definitions.definition: A meal or event highlighted by food cooked in such an apparatus.
definitions.example: We're having a barbecue on Saturday, and you're invited.

definitions.definition: Meat, especially pork or beef, which has been cooked in such an apparatus (i.e. smoked over indirect heat from high-smoke fuels) and then chopped up or shredded.
definitions.example: She ordered a plate of barbecue with a side of slaw.

definitions.definition: A hog, ox, or other large animal roasted or broiled whole for a feast.
A floor on which coffee beans are sun-dried.
A framework of sticks.
To cook food on a barbecue; to smoke it over indirect heat from high-smoke fuels.
To grill.
Insecure

Any of several rodents of either of the taxonomic families Hystricidae (Old World porcupines) or Erethizontidae (New World porcupines), both from the infraorder Hystricognathi, noted for their sharp spines or quills, which are raised when the animal is attacked or surprised.
(originally leet) To own, to defeat or dominate (someone or something, especially a game or someone playing a game).
The act of deciding.
A choice or judgement.
definitions.example: It is the decision of the court that movies are protected as free speech.

definitions.definition: Firmness of conviction.
definitions.example: After agonizing deliberations, they finally proceeded with decision.

definitions.definition: (chiefly combat sports) A result arrived at by the judges when there is no clear winner at the end of the contest.
definitions.example: He has won twice by knockout, once by decision.

definitions.definition: A win or a loss awarded to a pitcher.
To defeat an opponent by a decision of the judges, rather than by a knockout
A structural element resembling the hollow upper half of a sphere; a cupola
Anything shaped like an upset bowl, often used as a cover
definitions.example: a cake dome

definitions.definition: Head (uppermost part of one's body)
Head, oral sex
A building; a house; an edifice
Any erection resembling the dome or cupola of a building, such as the upper part of a furnace, the vertical steam chamber on the top of a boiler, etc.
A prism formed by planes parallel to a lateral axis which meet above in a horizontal edge, like the roof of a house; also, one of the planes of such a form
A tank, often made of glass, for keeping live fish or other aquatic animals.
A public place where live fish and other aquatic animals are exhibited.
Violating morality or decency; provoking indignation or affront.
Transgressing reasonable limits; extravagant, immoderate.
Shocking; exceeding conventional behaviour; provocative.
Fierce, violent.
An object designed to open and close a lock.
An object designed to fit between two other objects (such as a shaft and a wheel) in a mechanism and maintain their relative orientation.
A crucial step or requirement.
definitions.example: The key to solving this problem is persistence.

definitions.definition: A guide explaining the symbols or terminology of a map or chart; a legend.
definitions.example: The key says that A stands for the accounting department.

definitions.definition: A guide to the correct answers of a worksheet or test.
definitions.example: Some students cheated by using the answer key.

definitions.definition: One of several small, usually square buttons on a typewriter or computer keyboard, mostly corresponding to text characters.
definitions.example: Press the Escape key.

definitions.definition: In musical instruments, one of the valve levers used to select notes, such as a lever opening a hole on a woodwind.
In instruments with a keyboard such as an organ or piano, one of the levers, or especially the exposed front end of it, which are depressed to cause a particular sound or note to be produced.
The lowest note of a scale; keynote.
In musical theory, the total melodic and harmonic relations, which exist between the tones of an ideal scale, major or minor; tonality.
In musical theory and notation, the tonality centering in a given tone, or the several tones taken collectively, of a given scale, major or minor.
In musical notation, a sign at the head of a staff indicating the musical key.
The general pitch or tone of a sentence or utterance.
A modification of an advertisement so as to target a particular group or demographic.
An indehiscent, one-seeded fruit furnished with a wing, such as the fruit of the ash and maple; a samara.
A manual electrical switching device primarily used for the transmission of Morse code.
A piece of information (e.g. a passphrase) used to encode or decode a message or messages.
A password restricting access to an IRC channel.
In a relational database, a field used as an index into another table (not necessarily unique).
A value that uniquely identifies an entry in a container.
The free-throw lane together with the circle surrounding the free-throw line, the free-throw lane having formerly been narrower, giving the area the shape of a skeleton key hole.
definitions.example: He shoots from the top of the key.

definitions.definition: A series of logically organized groups of discriminating information which aims to allow the user to correctly identify a taxon.
A piece of wood used as a wedge.
The last board of a floor when laid down.
A keystone.
That part of the plastering which is forced through between the laths and holds the rest in place.
A wooden support for a rail on the bullhead rail system.
The degree of roughness, or retention ability of a surface to have applied a liquid such as paint, or glue.
definitions.example: The door panel should be sanded down carefully to provide a good key for the new paint.

definitions.definition: The thirty-third card of the Lenormand deck.
(print and film) The black ink layer, especially in relation to the three color layers of cyan, magenta, and yellow. See also CMYK.
A color to be masked or made transparent.
To fit (a lock) with a key.
To fit (pieces of a mechanical assembly) with a key to maintain the orientation between them.
To mark or indicate with a symbol indicating membership in a class.
(telegraphy and radio telegraphy) To depress (a telegraph key).
To operate (the transmitter switch of a two-way radio).
(more usually to key in) To enter (information) by typing on a keyboard or keypad.
definitions.example: Our instructor told us to key in our user IDs.

definitions.definition: To vandalize (a car, etc.) by scratching with an implement such as a key.
definitions.example: He keyed the car that had taken his parking spot.

definitions.definition: To link (as one might do with a key or legend).
To be identified as a certain taxon when using a key.
To modify (an advertisement) so as to target a particular group or demographic.
To attune to; to set at; to pitch.
To fasten or secure firmly; to fasten or tighten with keys or wedges.
Indispensable, supremely important.
definitions.example: He is the key player on his soccer team.

definitions.definition: Important, salient.
definitions.example: She makes several key points.

definitions.definition: Agent noun of pick; one who picks.
definitions.example: The apple picker climbed the tree.

definitions.definition: Any user interface control that selects something.
definitions.example: a date picker

definitions.definition: A machine for picking fibrous materials to pieces so as to loosen and separate the fibre.
The piece in a loom that strikes the end of the shuttle and impels it through the warp.
A priming wire for cleaning the vent, in ordnance.
(gold panning) A fragment of gold smaller than a nugget but large enough to be picked up.
One who removes defects from and finishes electrotype plates.
A pilferer.

A semi-aquatic, egg-laying monotreme mammal with a bill resembling that of a duck, that has a mole-like body, a tail resembling that of a beaver, a waterproof pelt, and flat webbed feet — males have poisonous spurs on the inside of the back legs; Ornithorhynchus anatinus
A soft moist mixture, in particular:
A substance that behaves as a solid until a sufficiently large load or stress is applied, at which point it flows like a fluid
A hard lead-containing glass, or an artificial gemstone made from this glass.
Pasta.
The mineral substance in which other minerals are embedded.
To stick with paste; to cause to adhere by or as if by paste.
To insert a piece of media (e.g. text, picture, audio, video) previously copied or cut from somewhere else.
To strike or beat someone or something.
To defeat decisively or by a large margin.
An infant born prematurely
Occurring before a state of readiness or maturity has arrived
definitions.example: a premature birth

definitions.definition: Taking place earlier than anticipated, prepared for, or desired
Suffering from premature ejaculation
A termination or conclusion.
The last part of something.
(grammar) The last morpheme of a word, added to some base to make an inflected form (such as -ing in "ending").
A coarse woolen fabric used for clothing.
To spend money, time, or energy on something, especially for some benefit or purpose; used with in.
definitions.example: We'd like to thank all the contributors who have invested countless hours into this event.

definitions.definition: To clothe or wrap (with garments).
To put on (clothing).
To envelop, wrap, cover.
To commit money or capital in the hope of financial gain.
To ceremonially install someone in some office.
To formally give (someone) some power or authority.
To formally give (power or authority).
To surround, accompany, or attend.
To lay siege to.
definitions.example: to invest a town

definitions.definition: To make investments.
To prepare for lost wax casting by creating an investment mold (a mixture of a silica sand and plaster).
To be involved in; to form strong attachments to.
The property of a person of having high ideals that are usually unrealizable or at odds with practical life.
The practice or habit of giving or attributing ideal form or character to things; treatment of things in art or literature according to ideal standards or patterns;—opposed to realism.
An approach to philosophical enquiry, which asserts that direct and immediate knowledge can only be had of ideas or mental pictures.
Exposed to the wind
In the direction from which the wind is blowing
A narcissist.
Having an inflated idea of one's own importance.
Obsessed with one's own self image and ego.
The art of performing acrobatic gymnastic feats.
A spectacular display of agility.
The state of being arrogant; a type of extreme or foolish pride in which someone feels much superior to another
Widely cultivated cereal grass, typically Avena sativa.
definitions.example: The main forms of oat are meal and bran.

definitions.definition: Any of the numerous species, varieties, or cultivars of any of several similar grain plants in genus Avena.
definitions.example: The wild red oat is thought to be the ancestor of modern food oats.

definitions.definition: (usually as plural) The seeds of the oat, a grain, harvested as a food crop.
A simple musical pipe made of oat-straw.
A mass of oat plants (genus Avena, especially Avena sativa).
definitions.example: Oats are in the north corner of the farm.

definitions.definition: Seeds of an oat plant, especially prepared as food.
definitions.example: Oats are good for you.

definitions.definition: An explosive device used or intended as a weapon.
A failure; an unpopular commercial product.
definitions.example: box office bomb

definitions.definition: A large amount of money.
definitions.example: cost a bomb

definitions.definition: (social) Something highly effective or attractive.
A cyclone whose central pressure drops at an average rate of at least one millibar per hour for at least 24 hours.
A heavy-walled container designed to permit chemical reactions under high pressure.
A great booming noise; a hollow sound.
A woman’s breast.
A professional wrestling throw in which an opponent is lifted and then slammed back-first down to the mat.
A recreational drug ground up, wrapped, and swallowed.
An act of jumping into water while keeping one's arms and legs tucked into the body, as in a squatting position, to maximize splashing.
To attack using one or more bombs; to bombard.
To fail dismally.
To jump into water in a squatting position, with the arms wrapped around the legs.
To sound; to boom; to make a humming or buzzing sound.
To cover an area in many graffiti tags.
To add an excessive amount of chlorine to a pool when it has not been maintained properly.
To make oneself drunk.
(especially with along, down, up etc.) To move at high speed.
definitions.example: I was bombing down the road on my motorbike.

definitions.definition: Great, awesome.
definitions.example: Have you tried the new tacos from that restaurant? They're pretty bomb!

definitions.definition: Someone who routes or directs items from one location to another.
definitions.example: The router directed the movement of the company's trucks.

definitions.definition: Any device that directs packets of information using the equivalent of Open Systems Interconnection layer 3 (network layer) information. Most commonly used in reference to Internet Protocol routers.

A device that connects local area networks to form a larger internet by, at minimum, selectively passing those datagrams having a destination IP address to the network which is able to deliver them to their destination; a network gateway.
definitions.example: The router was configured to forward packets outside of a certain range of IP addresses to its internet uplink port.

definitions.definition: (electronic design automation) In integrated circuit or printed circuit board design, an algorithm for adding all wires needed to properly connect all of the placed components while obeying all design rules.
A power tool used in carpentry for cutting grooves.
definitions.example: He made an attractive edge on the table with a router.

definitions.definition: A plane made like a spokeshave, for working the inside edges of circular sashes.
A plane with a hooked tool protruding far below the sole, for smoothing the bottom of a cavity.
To hollow out or cut using a router power tool.
Any of several American blackbirds of the genus Quiscalus, and related genera, having iridescent plumage.
(formerly) Any of several Asian myna birds of the genus Gracula.
Something that has an undefeatable quality.
Not defeatable; impossible to defeat.
(physical) Visual perception.
A picture, drawn or painted; a sketch.
definitions.example: a fine view of Lake George

definitions.definition: (psychological) Opinion, judgement, imagination.
A virtual or logical table composed of the result set of a query in relational databases.
The part of a computer program which is visible to the user and can be interacted with
A wake.
To look at.
definitions.example: The video was viewed by millions of people.

definitions.definition: To regard in a stated way.
definitions.example: I view it as a serious breach of trust.

definitions.definition: An instrument for measuring electric potential in volts.
At night.
definitions.example: I work nights.

definitions.definition: Every night.
Lacking a permanent place of residence.
definitions.example: Whenever I pass the park, I see the homeless people sleeping on the benches.

definitions.definition: A man, especially a warrior or hero.
A ring; a circle.
A sheet of ice prepared for playing certain sports, such as hockey or curling.
definitions.example: We played hockey all winter until the rink melted.

definitions.definition: A surface for roller skating.
A building housing an ice rink.
A team in a competition.
definitions.example: The Schmirler rink won the Silver Broom.

definitions.definition: To cast off capriciously or unfeelingly, as a lover; to deceive in love.
To drive mad; to craze
Insane or mentally ill.
Suffering from dementia.
Crazy; ridiculous.
definitions.example: a demented idea

definitions.definition: A small cut in a surface.
Senses connoting something small.
Often in the expressions in bad nick and in good nick: condition, state.
definitions.example: The car I bought was cheap and in good nick.

definitions.definition: (law enforcement) A police station or prison.
definitions.example: He was arrested and taken down to Sun Hill nick [police station] to be charged.

definitions.definition: To make a nick or notch in; to cut or scratch in a minor way.
definitions.example: I nicked myself while I was shaving.

definitions.definition: To fit into or suit, as by a correspondence of nicks; to tally with.
To make a cut at the side of the face.
To steal.
definitions.example: Someone’s nicked my bike!

definitions.definition: (law enforcement) To arrest.
definitions.example: The police nicked him climbing over the fence of the house he’d broken into.

definitions.definition: To give or call (someone) by a nickname; to style.
A nix or nixie.
A familiar, invented given name for a person or thing used instead of the actual name of the person or thing.
A kind of byname that describes a person by a characteristic of that person.
An impression; the mark left behind by printing something.
definitions.example: The day left an imprint in my mind.

definitions.definition: The name and details of a publisher or printer, as printed in a book etc.; a publishing house.
A distinctive marking, symbol or logo.
definitions.example: The shirts bore the company imprint on the right sleeve.

definitions.definition: An artificial obstruction, such as a dam, in a river designed to increase its depth or to divert its flow.
A heavy curtain of artillery fire directed in front of one's own troops to screen and protect them.
A concentrated discharge of projectile weapons.
(by extension) An overwhelming outburst of words, especially of criticism.
A "next hit wins" contest to determine the winner of a bout in case of a tie.
Type of firework containing a mixture of firework types in one single-ignition package.
To direct a barrage at.
A lucky or improbable occurrence, with the implication that the occurrence could not be repeated.
definitions.example: The first goal was just a fluke.

definitions.definition: To obtain a successful outcome by pure chance.
definitions.example: I fluked a pass in the multiple-choice exam.

definitions.definition: To fortuitously pot a ball in an unintended way.
definitions.example: He fluked the other red into the middle pocket, despite the double kiss.

definitions.definition: Lacking popularity
definitions.example: an unpopular opinion

definitions.definition: Not liked or popular; disliked or ignored by the public.
An advancement in rank or position.
definitions.example: I'll have to give myself a promotion!

definitions.definition: Dissemination of information in order to increase its popularity.
definitions.example: the promotion of the idea of global warming in schools

definitions.definition: An event intended to increase the reach or image of a product or brand.
definitions.example: The price cut is serving as a promotion of the manufacturer's new beverage varieties.

definitions.definition: Forward motion. (Contrast remotion.)
Unable to speak; lacking power of speech (kept in "deaf, dumb, and blind").
definitions.example: His younger brother was born dumb, and communicated with sign language.

definitions.definition: Silent; unaccompanied by words.
definitions.example: dumb show

definitions.definition: (especially of a person) Extremely stupid.
definitions.example: You are so dumb! You don't even know how to make toast!

definitions.definition: Pointless, foolish, lacking intellectual content or value.
definitions.example: Brendan had the dumb job of moving boxes from one conveyor belt to another.

definitions.definition: Lacking brightness or clearness, as a colour.
A glossy, resinous material used as a surface coating; either a natural exudation of certain trees, or a solution of nitrocellulose in alcohol, etc.
A similar finish, baked onto the inside of cans.
To apply a lacquer to something or to give something a smooth, glossy finish.
To look at.
definitions.example: The video was viewed by millions of people.

definitions.definition: To regard in a stated way.
definitions.example: I view it as a serious breach of trust.

definitions.definition: Having been viewed; having been seen, watched or witnessed.
The act of notifying.
A specific piece of information that serves to notify.
A text message on a cell phone.
The state of being an employee; employment.
definitions.example: The school district has six thousand teachers in its employ.

definitions.definition: The act of employing someone or making use of something; employment.
To hire (somebody for work or a job).
definitions.example: Yesterday our local garage employed a new mechanic.

definitions.definition: To use (somebody for a job, or something for a task).
definitions.example: The burglar employed a jemmy to get in.

definitions.definition: To make busy.
Distinctive atmosphere or quality associated with something.
An invisible force surrounding a living creature.
Perceptual disturbance experienced by some migraine sufferers before a migraine headache.
Telltale sensation experienced by some people with epilepsy before a seizure.
A closed container for liquids or gases.
An open container or pool for storing water or other liquids.
A pond, pool, or small lake, natural or artificial.
The fuel reservoir of a vehicle.
The amount held by a container; a tankful.
definitions.example: I burned three tanks of gas on the drive to New York.

definitions.definition: An armoured fighting vehicle, armed with a gun, and moving on caterpillar tracks.
(Australian and Indian English) A reservoir or dam.
A large metal container for holding drinking water for animals, usually placed near a wind-driven water pump, in an animal pen or field.
By extension a small pond for the same purpose.
A very muscular and physically imposing person. Somebody who is built like a tank.
A unit or character designed primarily around damage absorption and holding the attention of the enemy (as opposed to dealing damage, healing, or other tasks).
A prison cell, or prison generally.
A metaphorical place where a player goes to contemplate a decision; see in the tank.
To fail or fall (often used in describing the economy or the stock market); to degenerate or decline rapidly; to plummet.
To attract the attacks of an enemy target in cooperative team-based combat, so that one's teammates can defeat the enemy in question more efficiently.
To put (fuel, etc.) into a tank.
To deliberately lose a sports match with the intent of gaining a perceived future competitive advantage.
To resist damage; to be attacked without being hurt.
To contemplate a decision for a long time; to go in the tank.
A small Indian dry measure, averaging 240 grains in weight.
A Bombay weight of 72 grains, for pearls.
To stand; to tolerate.
(with “in”) To show to be connected or involved in an unfavorable or criminal way.
definitions.example: The evidence implicates involvement of top management in the scheme.

definitions.definition: To imply, to have as a necessary consequence or accompaniment.
definitions.example: What did Nixon's visit to China implicate for Russia?

definitions.definition: To imply without entailing; to have as an implicature.
To fold or twist together, intertwine, interlace, entangle, entwine.
A container, typically made of glass or plastic and having a tapered neck, used primarily for holding liquids.
definitions.example: Beer is often sold in bottles.

definitions.definition: The contents of such a container.
definitions.example: I only drank a bottle of beer.

definitions.definition: A container with a rubber nipple used for giving liquids to infants, a baby bottle.
definitions.example: The baby wants a bottle.

definitions.definition: Nerve, courage.
definitions.example: He was going to ask her out, but he lost his bottle when he saw her.

definitions.definition: (of a person with a particular hair color) A container of hair dye, hence with one’s hair color produced by dyeing.
definitions.example: Did you know he’s a bottle brunette? His natural hair color is strawberry blonde.

definitions.definition: A bundle, especially of hay; something tied in a bundle.
Intoxicating liquor; alcohol.
definitions.example: to drown one’s troubles in the bottle

definitions.definition: The tendency of pages printed several on a sheet to rotate slightly when the sheet is folded two or more times.
To seal (a liquid) into a bottle for later consumption. Also fig.
definitions.example: This plant bottles vast quantities of spring water every day.

definitions.definition: To feed (an infant) baby formula.
definitions.example: Because of complications she can't breast feed her baby and so she bottles him.

definitions.definition: To refrain from doing (something) at the last moment because of a sudden loss of courage.
definitions.example: The rider bottled the big jump.

definitions.definition: To throw away a leading position.
definitions.example: Liverpool bottled the Premier League.

definitions.definition: To strike (someone) with a bottle.
definitions.example: He was bottled at a nightclub and had to have facial surgery.

definitions.definition: To pelt (a musical act on stage, etc.) with bottles as a sign of disapproval.
definitions.example: Meat Loaf was once bottled at Reading Festival.

definitions.definition: A dwelling; habitation.
A building; house.
An act of winking (a blinking of only one eye), or a message sent by winking.
A brief period of sleep; especially forty winks.
A brief time; an instant.
The smallest possible amount.
A subtle allusion.
definitions.example: The film includes a wink to wartime rationing.

definitions.definition: To close one's eyes in sleep.
To close one's eyes.
Usually followed by at: to look the other way, to turn a blind eye.
To close one's eyes quickly and involuntarily; to blink.
To blink with only one eye as a message, signal, or suggestion, usually with an implication of conspiracy. (When transitive, the object may be the eye being winked, or the message being conveyed.)
definitions.example: He winked at me. She winked her eye. He winked his assent.

definitions.definition: To gleam fitfully or intermitently; to twinkle; to flicker.
A rattling metallic sound.
Idle talk; prate; chatter; babble.
To make a rattling metallic sound.
To cause something to make a rattling metallic sound.
To irritate.
definitions.example: The sound from the next apartment jangled my nerves.

definitions.definition: To quarrel in words; to wrangle.
(sometimes figurative) To free from the condition of being stuck.
To raise something or someone to a higher physical, social, moral, intellectual, spiritual or emotional level.
(of a penalty) To aggravate; to increase.
(travel) To be accepted for carriage on a flight.
To remove (a child) from a damaging home environment by a social welfare organization.
Lifted upwards, raised.
Joyous, hopeful or inspired.
A person who lives on other people's efforts or expense and gives little or nothing back.
A sycophant or hanger-on.

An organism that lives on or in another organism of a different species, deriving benefit from living on or in that other organism, while not contributing towards that other organism sufficiently to cover the cost to that other organism.
definitions.example: Lice, fleas, ticks and mites are widely spread parasites.

definitions.definition: A climbing plant which is supported by a wall, trellis etc.
A retainer or companion of an ancient Celtic warrior, who praised him in song or poetry at gatherings; a bard.
The state or condition of being prime or first, as in time, place, rank, etc.
definitions.example: December 25 2016, Amruta Patil writing in The Hindu, The book in my hand

definitions.definition: Excellence; supremacy.
The office, rank, or character of a primate, it being the chief ecclesiastical station or dignity in a national church
The office or dignity of an archbishop
A domestic cat breed with short legs.
The empty space in the center of a donut.
A small ball-shaped pastry, made in the same manner as a donut, roughly the size of the hole in a donut.
definitions.example: These donut holes are delicious!

definitions.definition: (welfare) An income bracket where one is without benefits, even though benefits are available in the next lower and next higher brackets.
A section of water for which no one has fishing rights that is completely surrounded by regions where various parties hold fishing rights.
A child.
A person of very short stature.
A player who mainly concentrates on increasing their character's power and capabilities.
Finely chopped.
Minutely subdivided.
Weakened, extenuated.

Any substance or drug that reduces pain, induces sleep and may alter mood or behaviour; in some contexts, especially in reference to the opiates-and-opioids class, especially in reference to illegal drugs, and often both.
Any type of numbing drug.
Of, or relating to narcotics.
(pharmaceutical effect) Inducing sleep; causing narcosis.
To exercise influence over; to suggest or dictate the behavior of.
definitions.example: With a simple remote, he could control the toy truck.

definitions.definition: (construed with for) To design (an experiment) so that the effects of one or more variables are reduced or eliminated.
The act of exerting control.
Exerting control over a person or thing.
definitions.example: His mother is very controlling.

definitions.definition: A vehicle set up to carry many people (now usually called a bus).
An anthology of previously released material linked together by theme or author, especially in book form.
A broadcast programme consisting of all of the episodes of a serial that have been shown in the previous week.
definitions.example: The omnibus edition of The Archers is broadcast every Sunday morning at 11.00.

definitions.definition: A stamp issue, usually commemorative, that appears simultaneously in several countries as a joint issue.
To combine (legislative bills, etc.) into a single package.
To drive an omnibus.
To travel or be transported by omnibus.
Containing multiple items.
definitions.example: The legislature enacted an omnibus appropriations bill.

definitions.definition: (usually uncountable) Any of a large number of different species of fish, which dwell among rocks, specifically:
A black person who does not know how to swim.
A quick, light sweeping motion.
definitions.example: With a quick whisk, she swept the cat from the pantry with her broom.

definitions.definition: A kitchen utensil, made from stiff wire loops fixed to a handle, used for whipping (or a mechanical device with the same function).
definitions.example: He used a whisk to whip up a light and airy souffle.

definitions.definition: A bunch of twigs or hair etc, used as a brush.
definitions.example: Peter dipped the whisk in lather and applied it to his face, so he could start shaving.

definitions.definition: A small handheld broom with a small (or no) handle.
definitions.example: I used a whisk to sweep the counter, then a push-broom for the floor.

definitions.definition: A plane used by coopers for evening chines.
A kind of cape, forming part of a woman's dress.
An impertinent fellow.
To move something with quick light sweeping motions.
definitions.example: Vernon whisked the sawdust from his workbench.

definitions.definition: In cooking, to whip e.g. eggs or cream.
definitions.example: The chef prepared to whisk the egg whites for the angel's food cake.

definitions.definition: To move something rapidly and with no warning.
definitions.example: The governess whisked the children from the room before they could see their presents.

definitions.definition: To move lightly and nimbly.
definitions.example: The children whisked down the road to the fair, laughing and chattering as they went.

definitions.definition: Offspring.
Lineage.
A difficult return or block of a shot.
Something gained; an acquisition.
(ditransitive) To obtain; to acquire.
definitions.example: I'm going to get a computer tomorrow from the discount store.

definitions.definition: To receive.
definitions.example: He got a severe reprimand for that.

definitions.definition: (in a perfect construction, with present-tense meaning) To have. See usage notes.
definitions.example: I've got a concert ticket for you.

definitions.definition: To fetch, bring, take.
definitions.example: Can you get my bag from the living-room, please?

definitions.definition: To become, or cause oneself to become.
definitions.example: I'm getting hungry; how about you?

definitions.definition: To cause to become; to bring about.
definitions.example: I can't get these boots off (or on).

definitions.definition: To cause to do.
definitions.example: I can't get it to work.

definitions.definition: To cause to come or go or move.
definitions.example: I got him to his room.

definitions.definition: To cause to be in a certain status or position.
definitions.example: Get him here at once.

definitions.definition: (with various prepositions, such as into, over, or behind; for specific idiomatic senses see individual entries get into, get over, etc.) To adopt, assume, arrive at, or progress towards (a certain position, location, state).
definitions.example: I'm getting into a muddle.

definitions.definition: To cover (a certain distance) while travelling.
definitions.example: to get a mile

definitions.definition: To begin (doing something or to do something).
definitions.example: After lunch we got chatting.

definitions.definition: To take or catch (a scheduled transportation service).
definitions.example: I normally get the 7:45 train.

definitions.definition: To respond to (a telephone call, a doorbell, etc).
definitions.example: Can you get that call, please? I'm busy.

definitions.definition: (followed by infinitive) To be able, be permitted, or have the opportunity (to do something desirable or ironically implied to be desirable).
definitions.example: Great. I get to clean the toilets today.

definitions.definition: To understand. (compare get it)
definitions.example: I don't get what you mean by "fun". This place sucks!

definitions.definition: To be told; be the recipient of (a question, comparison, opinion, etc.).
definitions.example: "You look just like Helen Mirren." / "I get that a lot."

definitions.definition: To be. Used to form the passive of verbs.
definitions.example: He got bitten by a dog.

definitions.definition: To become ill with or catch (a disease).
definitions.example: I went on holiday and got malaria.

definitions.definition: To catch out, trick successfully.
definitions.example: He keeps calling pretending to be my boss—it gets me every time.

definitions.definition: To perplex, stump.
definitions.example: That question's really got me.

definitions.definition: To find as an answer.
definitions.example: What did you get for question four?

definitions.definition: To bring to reckoning; to catch (as a criminal); to effect retribution.
definitions.example: I'm gonna get him for that.

definitions.definition: To hear completely; catch.
definitions.example: Sorry, I didn't get that. Could you repeat it?

definitions.definition: To getter.
definitions.example: I put the getter into the container to get the gases.

definitions.definition: To beget (of a father).
To learn; to commit to memory; to memorize; sometimes with out.
definitions.example: to get a lesson;  to get out one's Greek lesson

definitions.definition: Used with a personal pronoun to indicate that someone is being pretentious or grandiose.
definitions.example: Get her with her new hairdo.

definitions.definition: To go, to leave; to scram.
To kill.
definitions.example: They’re coming to get you, Barbara.

definitions.definition: To make acquisitions; to gain; to profit.
To measure.
definitions.example: Did you get her temperature?

definitions.definition: A git.
Something which is fair (in various senses of the adjective).
definitions.example: When will we learn to distinguish between the fair and the foul?

definitions.definition: A woman, a member of the ‘fair sex’; also as a collective singular, women.
Fairness, beauty.
A fair woman; a sweetheart.
Good fortune; good luck.
To smoothen or even a surface (especially a connection or junction on a surface).
To bring into perfect alignment (especially about rivet holes when connecting structural members).
To construct or design a structure whose primary function is to produce a smooth outline or reduce air drag or water resistance.
To make fair or beautiful.
Beautiful, of a pleasing appearance, with a pure and fresh quality.
definitions.example: Monday's child is fair of face.

definitions.definition: Unblemished (figuratively or literally); clean and pure; innocent.
definitions.example: After scratching out and replacing various words in the manuscript, he scribed a fair copy to send to the publisher.

definitions.definition: Light in color, pale, particularly with regard to skin tone but also referring to blond hair.
definitions.example: She had fair hair and blue eyes.

definitions.definition: Just, equitable.
definitions.example: He must be given a fair trial.

definitions.definition: Adequate, reasonable, or decent.
definitions.example: The patient was in a fair condition after some treatment.

definitions.definition: (of a wind) Favorable to a ship's course.
Not overcast; cloudless; clear; pleasant; propitious; said of the sky, weather, or wind, etc.
definitions.example: a fair sky;  a fair day

definitions.definition: Free from obstacles or hindrances; unobstructed; unencumbered; open; direct; said of a road, passage, etc.
definitions.example: a fair mark;  in fair sight;  a fair view

definitions.definition: Without sudden change of direction or curvature; smooth; flowing; said of the figure of a vessel, and of surfaces, water lines, and other lines.
Between the baselines.
(of a catch) Taken direct from an opponent's foot, without the ball touching the ground or another player.
(of a ball delivered by the bowler) Not a no-ball.
Of a coin or die, having equal chance of landing on any side, unbiased.
Clearly, openly, frankly, civilly, honestly, favorably, auspiciously, agreeably
A natural substance that is used to make the ground more suitable for growing plants.
A chemical compound created to have the same effect.
A trading establishment, especially set up by merchants working in a foreign country.
The position or state of being a factor.
A building or other place where manufacturing takes place.
A device which produces or manufactures something.
A factory farm.
definitions.example: chicken factory; pig factory

definitions.definition: In a computer program or library, a function, method, etc. which creates an object.
A police station.
The ground near the edge of a lake; the land adjacent to a lake.
By the side of a lake.
The bell that summons worshipers to vespers; the vesper-bell
The evening.
A vesper martini.
To grind or otherwise process in a mill or other machine.
definitions.example: to mill flour

definitions.definition: To shape, polish, dress or finish using a machine.
To engrave one or more grooves or a pattern around the edge of (a cylindrical object such as a coin).
(followed by around, about, etc.) To move about in an aimless fashion.
definitions.example: I didn't have much to do, so I just milled around the town looking at the shops.

definitions.definition: To cause to mill, or circle around.
definitions.example: to mill cattle

definitions.definition: (of air-breathing creatures) To swim underwater.
(of a whale) To swim suddenly in a new direction.
To beat; to pound.
To pass through a fulling mill; to full, as cloth.
To roll (steel, etc.) into bars.
To make (drinking chocolate) frothy, as by churning.
To undergo hulling.
definitions.example: This maize mills well.

definitions.definition: To take part in a fistfight; to box.
To fill (a winze or interior incline) with broken ore, to be drawn out at the bottom.
(thieves' cant) To commit burglary.
(trading card games) To move (a card) from a deck to the discard pile.
(Hearthstone) To destroy (a card) due to having a full hand.
A grinding process using a mill.

The series of notches around the edge of a coin, placed there during minting so that it can be told if some of the metal from the edge is removed. Removing metal from a coin was common practice during earlier times when coins made of precious metals circulated.
A circular or random motion of a herd or a crowd.
Demeanor; facial expression or attitude, especially one which is intended by its bearer.
A specific facial expression.
An excavation from which ore or solid minerals are taken, especially one consisting of underground tunnels.
Any source of wealth or resources.
A passage dug toward or underneath enemy lines, which is then packed with explosives.
A device intended to explode when stepped upon or touched, or when approached by a ship, vehicle, or person.
A type of firework that explodes on the ground, shooting sparks upward.
The cavity made by a caterpillar while feeding inside a leaf.
A machine or network of machines used to extract units of a cryptocurrency.
To remove (ore) from the ground.
To dig into, for ore or metal.
To sow mines (the explosive devices) in (an area).
To damage (a vehicle or ship) with a mine (an explosive device).
To dig a tunnel or hole; to burrow in the earth.
To dig away, or otherwise remove, the substratum or foundation of; to lay a mine under; to sap; to undermine.
(by extension) To ruin or destroy by slow degrees or secret means.
To pick one's nose.
To earn new units of cryptocurrency by doing certain calculations.
Variation of mine, formed through analogy based on the final -s of the other possessive pronouns (ours, yours, hers, theirs, his)
The unordered state of matter in classical accounts of cosmogony.
Any state of disorder; a confused or amorphous mixture or conglomeration.
definitions.example: After the earthquake, the local hospital was in chaos

definitions.definition: A behaviour of iterative non-linear systems in which arbitrarily small variations in initial conditions become magnified over time.
One of the two metaphysical forces of the world in some fantasy settings, as opposed to law.
A vast chasm or abyss.
A given medium; a space in which something exists or lives; an environment.
One who shoots an arrow from a bow or a bolt from a crossbow.
The bishop in chess.
To inspire boredom in somebody.
To make a hole through something.
To make a hole with, or as if with, a boring instrument; to cut a circular hole by the rotary motion of a tool.
definitions.example: An insect bores into a tree.

definitions.definition: To form or enlarge (something) by means of a boring instrument or apparatus.
definitions.example: to bore a steam cylinder or a gun barrel; to bore a hole

definitions.definition: To make (a passage) by laborious effort, as in boring; to force a narrow and difficult passage through.
definitions.example: to bore one's way through a crowd

definitions.definition: To be pierced or penetrated by an instrument that cuts as it turns.
definitions.example: This timber does not bore well.

definitions.definition: To push forward in a certain direction with laborious effort.
(of a horse) To shoot out the nose or toss it in the air.
To fool; to trick.
Suffering from boredom; mildly annoyed and restless through having nothing to do
Uninterested, without attention
definitions.example: The piano teacher's bored look indicated he wasn't paying much attention to his pupil's lackluster rendition of Mozart's Requiem

definitions.definition: Perforated by a hole or holes (through bioerosion or other)
To dig, chop, etc., with a bill.
To peck
To stroke bill against bill, with reference to doves; to caress in fondness
To advertise by a bill or public notice.
To charge; to send a bill to.
Having a specified kind of bill (beak or beak-like projection).
To go in the reverse direction.
definitions.example: the train backed into the station;  the horse refuses to back

definitions.definition: To support.
definitions.example: I back you all the way;  which horse are you backing in this race?

definitions.definition: (of the wind) To change direction contrary to the normal pattern; that is, to shift anticlockwise in the northern hemisphere, or clockwise in the southern hemisphere.
(of a square sail) To brace the yards so that the wind presses on the front of the sail, to slow the ship.
(of an anchor) To lay out a second, smaller anchor to provide additional holding power.
(of a hunting dog) To stand still behind another dog which has pointed.
To push or force backwards.
definitions.example: The mugger backed her into a corner and demanded her wallet.

definitions.definition: To get upon the back of; to mount.
To place or seat upon the back.
To make a back for; to furnish with a back.
definitions.example: to back books

definitions.definition: To adjoin behind; to be at the back of.
To write upon the back of, possibly as an endorsement.
definitions.example: to back a letter;  to back a note or legal document

definitions.definition: (of a justice of the peace) To sign or endorse (a warrant, issued in another county, to apprehend an offender).
To row backward with (oars).
definitions.example: to back the oars

definitions.definition: Support, especially financial.
definitions.example: It's a volunteer organization that works with backing from the city and a few grants.

definitions.definition: A liner or other material added behind or underneath.
definitions.example: The cardboard backing gives the notebook a little extra stiffness.

definitions.definition: A backdrop.
Musicians and vocalists who support the main performer.
The mounting of a horse or other animal.
The action of putting something back; a switching into reverse.
Providing support for the main performer.
